findings.title: Potential Underflow in effectiveSupply Calculation
findings.description: In AbstractYieldStrategy.sol, the effectiveSupply() function calculates (totalSupply() - s_escrowedShares + VIRTUAL_SHARES). If s_escrowedShares exceeds totalSupply(), this could underflow, causing incorrect share calculations and potential fund loss.
findings.recommendation: Add a check to ensure s_escrowedShares does not exceed totalSupply() before performing the subtraction. Consider using uint256 with explicit overflow checks or using SafeMath library.

findings.title: Taylor Approximation Overflow in Fee Calculation
findings.description: In AbstractYieldStrategy.sol, _calculateAdditionalFeesInYieldToken() uses Taylor approximation (1 + x + x^2/2! + x^3/3!) which can overflow if x becomes large. The x value is calculated as (feeRate * timeSinceLastFeeAccrual) / YEAR, which could grow unbounded over time.
findings.recommendation: Add overflow checks before the Taylor approximation calculation. Consider using a bounded time window or capping the fee calculation to prevent overflow.

findings.title: Oracle Manipulation in Price Conversion
findings.description: In AbstractYieldStrategy.sol, convertYieldTokenToAsset() calls TRADING_MODULE.getOraclePrice() without any validation or deviation checks. An attacker could manipulate oracle prices to affect yield token valuations.
findings.recommendation: Implement oracle deviation checks similar to AbstractLPOracle.sol. Add multiple oracle sources and require consensus before accepting price data.

findings.title: Delegatecall Risk in Trade Execution
findings.description: In AbstractYieldStrategy.sol, _executeTrade() uses delegatecall to TRADING_MODULE implementation. If the implementation contract is compromised or malicious, it could access the vault's storage and state.
findings.recommendation: Use staticcall instead of delegatecall if possible. Implement strict access controls on the trading module. Consider using a proxy pattern with immutable implementation addresses.

findings.title: Front-running Vulnerability in Trade Execution
findings.description: In AbstractSingleSidedLP.sol, _executeDepositTrades() and _executeRedemptionTrades() execute trades with block.timestamp as deadline. This allows MEV bots to front-run vault trades, potentially causing slippage and fund loss.
findings.recommendation: Implement TWAP (Time-Weighted Average Price) or use limit orders with longer deadlines. Consider using private transaction pools or batch execution to reduce front-running risk.

findings.title: Oracle Manipulation in Withdraw Request Valuation
findings.description: In AbstractWithdrawRequestManager.sol, getWithdrawRequestValue() uses TRADING_MODULE.getOraclePrice() to value withdraw requests. This allows oracle manipulation to affect liquidation values and withdraw request valuations.
findings.recommendation: Implement multiple oracle sources with deviation checks. Consider using TWAP oracles for more stable price feeds. Add minimum/maximum price bounds.

findings.title: Revert in onMorphoRepay Could Cause Fund Loss
findings.description: In MorphoLendingRouter.sol, onMorphoRepay() reverts if assetsWithdrawn < assetToRepay after already redeeming yield tokens. This could leave funds stuck in the contract and cause partial loss.
findings.recommendation: Move the check before redeeming yield tokens. Consider implementing a refund mechanism for partial repayments. Add a minimum profit threshold before allowing the transaction to complete.

findings.title: Reentrancy in checkReentrancyContext
findings.description: In CurveConvex2Token.sol, checkReentrancyContext() calls Curve pool remove_liquidity methods which could be reentrant. While the function is called from nonReentrant contexts, the pattern is inconsistent.
findings.recommendation: Ensure all external calls to Curve pools are protected with ReentrancyGuardTransient. Consider using the Curve pool's built-in reentrancy protection mechanisms.

findings.title: Centralization Risk in Lending Router Position Entry
findings.description: In AddressRegistry.sol, setPosition() allows any whitelisted lending router to enter positions on any vault, including malicious vaults. This centralizes control and could be exploited.
findings.recommendation: Implement additional checks to validate vault legitimacy before allowing position entry. Consider requiring vault whitelisting at the protocol level rather than per-lending-router.

findings.title: Timestamp Dependence in Fee Accrual
findings.description: In AbstractYieldStrategy.sol, _accrueFees() uses block.timestamp for fee calculations. This creates timestamp dependence that could be exploited by miners to manipulate fee accrual timing.
findings.recommendation: Use block.timestamp with a minimum time delta check. Consider using TWAP oracles for more stable time-based calculations. Add a minimum time between fee accruals.

findings.title: Missing Slippage Protection in Instant Redemption
findings.description: In PendlePT_sUSDe.sol, the first leg of the sDAI trade has limit set to 0, relying on the second leg for slippage protection. This creates a window for price manipulation between trades.
findings.recommendation: Set appropriate slippage limits on all trade legs. Consider executing trades atomically or using a single multi-hop trade to reduce manipulation risk.

findings.title: Unchecked External Call Return Data
findings.description: In AbstractYieldStrategy.sol, _delegateCall() uses assembly to copy returndata but doesn't validate the return data format before use. Malicious implementations could return invalid data.
findings.recommendation: Add validation checks for return data format and length. Use abi.decode with proper error handling. Consider using SafeERC20 patterns for external calls.

findings.title: Upgrade Admin Key Concentration
findings.description: In AddressRegistry.sol, upgradeAdmin has extensive powers including pausing contracts, transferring admin roles, and setting fee receivers. Single key concentration creates centralization risk.
findings.recommendation: Implement multi-signature governance for upgrade admin. Consider using a timelock for critical admin actions. Add emergency pause mechanisms with separate controls.

findings.title: Token Handling Edge Effects Not Checked
findings.description: In AbstractYieldStrategy.sol, _transferYieldTokenToOwner() uses safeTransfer without checking for fee-on-transfer or rebasing tokens. This could cause unexpected token loss.
findings.recommendation: Add checks for fee-on-transfer tokens. Consider using IERC20Metadata to verify token compliance. Implement fallback mechanisms for non-standard tokens.

findings.title: Uninitialized Storage Variable Risk
findings.description: In AbstractYieldStrategy.sol, s_lastFeeAccrualTime is initialized in _initialize() but if initialization fails or is skipped, the variable remains 0, causing incorrect fee calculations.
findings.recommendation: Add initialization checks in constructor or require proper initialization before use. Consider using a separate initialization flag to track contract state.
